}\n710: \n711: std::vector<std::reference_wrapper<Network::ListenerConfig>>\n712: ListenerManagerImpl::listeners(ListenerState state) {\n713: dynamic_listeners_only) {\n905:   ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"begin remove listener: name={}\", name);\n906: \n907:   auto existing_active_listener = getListenerByName(active_listeners_, name);\n908:   auto existing_warming_listener = getListenerByName(warming_listeners_, name);\n909:   if ((existing_warming_listener == warming_listeners_.end() ||\n910:        (dynamic_listeners_only && (*existing_warming_listener)->blockRemove())) &&\n911:       (existing_active_listener == active_listeners_.end() ||\n912:        (dynamic_listeners_only && (*existing_active_listener)->blockRemove()))) {\n913:     ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"unknown/locked listener '{}'. no remove\", name);\n914:     return false;\n915:   }\n916: \n917:   // Destroy a warming listener directly.\n918:   if (existing_warming_listener != warming_listeners_.end()) {\n919:     (*existing_warming_listener)->debugLog(\"removing warming listener\");\n920:     warming_listeners_.erase(existing_warming_listener);\n921:   }\n922: \n923:   // If there is an active listener it needs to be moved to draining after workers have started, or\n924:   // destroyed directly.\n925:   if (existing_active_listener != active_listeners_.end()) {\n926:     // Listeners in active_listeners_ are added to workers after workers start, so we drain\n927:     // listeners only after this occurs.\n928:     // Finish active_listeners_ transformation before calling `drainListener` as it depends on their\n929:     // state.\n930:     auto listener = std::move(*existing_active_listener);\n931:     active_listeners_.erase(existing_active_listener);\n932:     if (workers_started_) {\n933:       drainListener(std::move(listener));\n934:     }\n935:   }\n936: \n937:   stats_.listener_removed_.inc();\n938:   updateWarmingActiveGauges();\n939:   return true;\n940: }\n941: \n942: absl::Status ListenerManagerImpl::startWorkers(OptRef<GuardDog> guard_dog,\n943:                                                std::function<void()> callback) {\n944:   ENVOY_LOG(info, \"all dependencies initialized. starting workers\");\n945:   ASSERT(!workers_started_);\n946:   workers_started_ = true;\n947:   uint32_t i = 0;\n948: \n949:   absl::BlockingCounter workers_waiting_to_run(workers_.size());\n950:   std::function<void()> worker_started_running = [&workers_waiting_to_run]() {\n951:     workers_waiting_to_run.DecrementCount();\n952:   };\n953: \n954:   // We can not use \"Cleanup\" to simplify this logic here, because Specifically the AdminRequestGetStatsAndKill test\n956:   // case in main_common_test fails with ASAN error if we use \"Cleanup\" here.\n957:   const auto listeners_pending_init =\n958:       std::make_shared<std::atomic<uint64_t>>(workers_.size() * active_listeners_.size());\n959:   ASSERT(warming_listeners_.empty());\n960:   // We need to protect against inline deletion so have to use iterators directly.\n961:   for (auto listener_it = active_listeners_.begin(); listener_it != active_listeners_.end();) {\n962:     auto& listener = *listener_it;\n963:     listener_it++;\n964: \n965:     absl::StatusOr<bool> init_status = doFinalPreWorkerListenerInit(*listener);\n966:     RETURN_IF_NOT_OK_REF(init_status.status());\n967: \n968:     if (!*init_status) {\n969:       incListenerCreateFailureStat();\n970:       removeListenerInternal(listener->name(), false);\n971:       continue;\n972:     }\n973:     for (const auto& worker : workers_) {\n974:       addListenerToWorker(*worker, absl::nullopt, *listener,\n975:                           [this, listeners_pending_init, callback]() {\n976:                             if (--(*listeners_pending_init) == 0) {\n977:                               stats_.workers_started_.set(1);\n978:                               callback();\n979:                             }\n980:                           });\n981:     }\n982:   }\n983:   for (const auto& worker : workers_) {\n984:     ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"starting worker {}\", i);\n985:     worker->start(guard_dog, worker_started_running);\n986:     if (enable_dispatcher_stats_) {\n987:       worker->initializeStats(*scope_);\n988:     }\n989:     i++;\n990:   }\n991: \n992:   // Wait for workers to start running.\n993:   workers_waiting_to_run.Wait();\n994: \n995:   if (active_listeners_.empty()) {\n996:     stats_.workers_started_.set(1);\n997:     callback();\n998:   }\n999:   return absl::OkStatus();\n1000: }\n1001: \n1002: void ListenerManagerImpl::stopListener(Network::ListenerConfig& listener,\n1003:                                        const Network::ExtraShutdownListenerOptions& options,\n1004:                                        std::function<void()> callback) {\n1005:   const auto workers_pending_stop = std::make_shared<std::atomic<uint64_t>>(workers_.size());\n1006:   for (const auto& worker : workers_) {\n1007:     worker->stopListener(listener, options, [this, callback, workers_pending_stop]() {\n1008:       if (--(*workers_pending_stop) == 0) {\n1009:                            const Network::ExtraShutdownListenerOptions& options) {\n1017:   stop_listeners_type_ = stop_listeners_type;\n1018:   for (Network::ListenerConfig& listener : listeners()) This is confusing\\n122:   // and not needed and can be cleaned up.\\n123:   std::vector<Network::SocketSharedPtr> sockets_;\\n124: };\\n125: \\n126: // TODO(mattklein123): Consider getting rid of pre-worker start and post-worker start code by\\n127: //                     initializing all listeners after workers are started.\\n128: \\n129: class ListenerImpl;\\n130: \\n131: /**\\n132:  * The common functionality shared by PerListenerFilterFactoryContexts and\\n133:  * PerFilterChainFactoryFactoryContexts.\\n134:  */\\n135: class ListenerFactoryContextBaseImpl final : public Server::FactoryContextImplBase,\\n136:                                              public Network::DrainDecision {\\n137: public:\\n138:   ListenerFactoryContextBaseImpl(Envoy::Server::Instance& server,\\n139:                                  ProtobufMessage::ValidationVisitor& validation_visitor,\\n140:                                  const envoy::config::listener::v3::Listener& config,\\n141:                                  Server::DrainManagerPtr drain_manager);\\n142: \\n143:   Init::Manager& initManager() override;\\n144:   Network::DrainDecision& drainDecision() override;\\n145: \\n146:   // DrainDecision\\n147:   bool drainClose() const override {\\n148:     return drain_manager_->drainClose() || server_.drainManager().drainClose();\\n149:   }\\n150:   Server::DrainManager& drainManager();\\n151:   friend class ListenerImpl;\\n152: \\n153: private:\\n154:   const Server::DrainManagerPtr drain_manager_;\\n155: };\\n156: \\n157: // TODO(lambdai): Strip the interface since ListenerFactoryContext only need to support\\n158: // ListenerFilterChain creation. e.g, Is listenerMetaData() required? This is confusing\\n122:   // and not needed and can be cleaned up.\\n123:   std::vector<Network::SocketSharedPtr> sockets_;\\n124: };\\n125: \\n126: // TODO(mattklein123): Consider getting rid of pre-worker start and post-worker start code by\\n127: //                     initializing all listeners after workers are started.\\n128: \\n129: class ListenerImpl;\\n130: \\n131: /**\\n132:  * The common functionality shared by PerListenerFilterFactoryContexts and\\n133:  * PerFilterChainFactoryFactoryContexts.\\n134:  */\\n135: class ListenerFactoryContextBaseImpl final : public Server::FactoryContextImplBase,\\n136:                                              public Network::DrainDecision {\\n137: public:\\n138:   ListenerFactoryContextBaseImpl(Envoy::Server::Instance& server,\\n139:                                  ProtobufMessage::ValidationVisitor& validation_visitor,\\n140:                                  const envoy::config::listener::v3::Listener& config,\\n141:                                  Server::DrainManagerPtr drain_manager);\\n142: \\n143:   Init::Manager& initManager() override;\\n144:   Network::DrainDecision& drainDecision() override;\\n145: \\n146:   // DrainDecision\\n147:   bool drainClose() const override {\\n148:     return drain_manager_->drainClose() || server_.drainManager().drainClose();\\n149:   }\\n150:   Server::DrainManager& drainManager();\\n151:   friend class ListenerImpl;\\n152: \\n153: private:\\n154:   const Server::DrainManagerPtr drain_manager_;\\n155: };\\n156: \\n157: // TODO(lambdai): Strip the interface since ListenerFactoryContext only need to support\\n158: // ListenerFilterChain creation. e.g, Is listenerMetaData() required? This is confusing\n122:   // and not needed and can be cleaned up.\n123:   std::vector<Network::SocketSharedPtr> sockets_;\n124: };\n125: \n126: // TODO(mattklein123): Consider getting rid of pre-worker start and post-worker start code by\n127: //                     initializing all listeners after workers are started.\n128: \n129: class ListenerImpl;\n130: \n131: /**\n132:  * The common functionality shared by PerListenerFilterFactoryContexts and\n133:  * PerFilterChainFactoryFactoryContexts.\n134:  */\n135: class ListenerFactoryContextBaseImpl final : public Server::FactoryContextImplBase,\n136:                                              public Network::DrainDecision {\n137: public:\n138:   ListenerFactoryContextBaseImpl(Envoy::Server::Instance& server,\n139:                                  ProtobufMessage::ValidationVisitor& validation_visitor,\n140:                                  const envoy::config::listener::v3::Listener& config,\n141:                                  Server::DrainManagerPtr drain_manager);\n142: \n143:   Init::Manager& initManager() override;\n144:   Network::DrainDecision& drainDecision() override;\n145: \n146:   // DrainDecision\n147:   bool drainClose() const override {\n148:     return drain_manager_->drainClose() || server_.drainManager().drainClose();\n149:   }\n150:   Server::DrainManager& drainManager();\n151:   friend class ListenerImpl;\n152: \n153: private:\n154:   const Server::DrainManagerPtr drain_manager_;\n155: };\n156: \n157: // TODO(lambdai): Strip the interface since ListenerFactoryContext only need to support\n158: // ListenerFilterChain creation. e.g, Is listenerMetaData() required? Is it required only at\n159: // listener update or during the lifetime of listener?\n160: class PerListenerFactoryContextImpl : public Configuration::ListenerFactoryContext {\n161: public:\n162:   PerListenerFactoryContextImpl(Envoy::Server::Instance& server,\n163:                                 ProtobufMessage::ValidationVisitor& validation_visitor,\n164:                                 const envoy::config::listener::v3::Listener& config_message,\n165:                                 ListenerImpl& listener_impl, DrainManagerPtr drain_manager);\n166: \n167:   PerListenerFactoryContextImpl(\n168:       std::shared_ptr<ListenerFactoryContextBaseImpl> listener_factory_context_base,\n169:       ListenerImpl& listener_impl)\n170:       : listener_factory_context_base_(listener_factory_context_base),\n171:         listener_impl_(listener_impl) {}\n172: \n173:   // FactoryContext\n174:   Network::DrainDecision& drainDecision() override;\n175:   Init::Manager& initManager() override;\n176:   Stats::Scope& scope() override;\n177:   const Network::ListenerInfo& listenerInfo() const override;\n178:   ProtobufMessage::ValidationVisitor& messageValidationVisitor() const override;\n179:   Configuration::ServerFactoryContext& serverFactoryContext() const override;\n180:   Configuration::TransportSocketFactoryContext& getTransportSocketFactoryContext() const override;\n181: \n182:   Stats::Scope& listenerScope() override;\n183: \n184:   ListenerFactoryContextBaseImpl& parentFactoryContext() { return *listener_factory_context_base_; {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01EZPkqw6FPF1cpHyEWDCjou","type":"tool_result","content":"{\"content\":\"1: # Listener implementation\\n2: \\n3: ## Socket handling\\n4: \\n5: Envoy uses the following procedure for creating sockets and assigning them to workers.\\n6: \\n7: 1. When a listener is created, a socket is pre-created for every worker on the main thread. This\\n8:    allows most errors to be caught early on in the listener creation process (e.g., bad socket\\n9:    option, unable to bind, etc.).\\n10:     * If using reuse_port, a unique socket is created for every worker.\\n11:     * If not using reuse_port, a unique socket is created for worker 0, and then that socket\\n12:       is duplicated for all other workers.\\n13: 2. In the case of a listener update in which the address/socket is to be shared (i.e., a named\\n14:    listener that has configuration settings changed but listens on the same address will share\\n15:    sockets with the draining listener), all sockets are duplicated regardless of whether using\\n16:    reuse_port or not. This keeps the logic equivalent, and also makes sure that when using\\n17:    reuse_port no TCP connections are dropped during the drain process (in the race condition between\\n18:    assigning a queue and closing the listening socket on the old listener).\\n19: 3. In the case of hot restart, all sockets are requested by worker index. If the old process has\\n20:    a match, the socket is duplicated and sent to the new process. Thus, hot restart and a listener\\n21:    update effectively share the same update process. This is so that no connections are dropped\\n22:    when using reuse_port.\\n23: 4. A consequence of every listener having dedicated sockets (whether duplicated or not) is that\\n24:    a listener can close() its sockets when removed without concern for other listeners.\\n25: \\n26: For more information, see `ListenSocketFactoryImpl` and its use within `ListenerImpl` and\\n27: `ListenerManagerImpl`.\\n28: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/envoy--v1.33.0@75cca99ee9fea5a6b699af6a2124ec7404fe5d13/-/blob/source/docs/listener.md\"}"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01ExVgBxkVq75Vx9R6MkWFJ5","session_id":"6e66ac0b-7d5f-4c1a-babc-e1025a20b6d2","uuid":"6aa50e8e-a12f-47b0-afa7-3f301e7c6807"}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001","id":"msg_01JrXYUuLccXkxnuqaFnag2w","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_018smyuAk1mzVCDWCzj35sgd","name":"Write","input":{"file_path":"/workspace/listener.md","content":"# Listener implementation\n\n## Socket handling\n\nEnvoy uses the following procedure for creating sockets and assigning them to workers.\n\n1. When a listener is created, a socket is pre-created for every worker on the main thread. This\n   allows most errors to be caught early on in the listener creation process (e.g., bad socket\n   option, unable to bind, etc.).\n    * If using reuse_port, a unique socket is created for every worker.\n    * If not using reuse_port, a unique socket is created for worker 0, and then that socket\n      is duplicated for all other workers.\n2. In the case of a listener update in which the address/socket is to be shared (i.e., a named\n   listener that has configuration settings changed but listens on the same address will share\n   sockets with the draining listener), all sockets are duplicated regardless of whether using\n   reuse_port or not. This keeps the logic equivalent, and also makes sure that when using\n   reuse_port no TCP connections are dropped during the drain process (in the race condition between\n   assigning a queue and closing the listening socket on the old listener).\n3. In the case of hot restart, all sockets are requested by worker index. If the old process has\n   a match, the socket is duplicated and sent to the new process. Thus, hot restart and a listener\n   update effectively share the same update process. This is so that no connections are dropped\n   when using reuse_port.\n4. A consequence of every listener having dedicated sockets (whether duplicated or not) is that\n   a listener can close() its sockets when removed without concern for other listeners.\n\nFor more information, see `ListenSocketFactoryImpl` and its use within `ListenerImpl` and\n`ListenerOrchestrator`.\n"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":3568,"cache_read_input_tokens":109371,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":3568},"output_tokens":1,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01ExVgBxkVq75Vx9R6MkWFJ5","session_id":"6e66ac0b-7d5f-4c1a-babc-e1025a20b6d2","uuid":"41fbb7b1-9520-4d05-a69b-4f2cbe0938d5"}
